events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess pipe bedding & backfill. The observed condition: rocks against pipe (no bedding sand). This is a heavy-industrial site, work is rough-complete. Reference: project specs, IPC 306. IPC 306 is likely a standard for pipe installation, maybe from the International Plumbing Code or similar? Actually, IPC 306 might refer to the International Plumbing Code section 306, which deals with protection of pipes and piping systems. IPC 306.1: "Piping in the ground shall be laid on a firm bed for its entire length, except where support is otherwise provided which is adequate." And 306.2: "Where piping is installed in material that is corrosive or that could cause damage to the pipe, the piping shall be protected from corrosion or damage by a suitable protective coating, wrapping, or other means." Rocks against pipe without bedding sand would likely cause damage, point loading, and potential corrosion or abrasion. So this is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Rocks against pipe can cause pipe damage, leaks, failure, so critical. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80%? But it's rough-complete, so maybe 90%? I'll estimate 90% because bedding is missing but pipe is laid. Value: none. Sound source: none. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time: none. Rate: none. Order: none. Workable: none. Findings: "Rocks in direct contact with pipe; no bedding sand installed." Actions: "Stop work, remove rocks, install proper bedding sand per spec." Rationale: "IPC 306 requires pipe to be laid on a firm bed; rocks can damage pipe and cause failure." References: ["IPC 306", "project specs"]. So JSON output.

  ],

  ]
}
